summary:
No FDA-approved drug therapy exists specifically for general NAFLD; however, some drugs have obtained accelerated approval under specific conditions related to MASLD subtypes such as advanced fibrosis. The primary management strategy currently involves lifestyle modification due to lack of formal pharmacologic options. 

Note that this summary is strictly based only on Agent1’s stated reasoning from Round 1 — no external information was included beyond what was provided above.

No FDA-approved therapies are available for general NAFLD, though accelerated approvals exist for select cases involving severe fibrosis within metabolically associated steatohepatitis (MASLD). Current care focuses on lifestyle changes because effective medications remain unapproved overall.
